# Transcrição: 05-How To Set Up Reporting Columns

- **Fonte:** 05-How To Set Up Reporting Columns
- **Duração:** 02:54

## [00:00 - 02:54]

Alright guys, so let's set up our reporting column so that every time we log into the ad account, we can see what we need to see at any one time. So when we're running the rapid fire test, you can just leave the reporting column on default because it's going to show you message replies and it's also going to show you cost per message. If you want to see the click through rate and the cost per click, you'll click on performance and clicks and it will bring up the information that you need. Once you activate your funnel, so let's say you're rolling out a landing page funnel or even a mini chat, you're going to want to make sure that you pull up the right information. Your reporting column is always showing you what you need to see, which is a lot of it is going to be your standard events, right? How many leads came through? So if you are going to be running a funnel where you are sending people to a landing page, what we can do here is we can go customize columns and you're going to be using the lead standard event in your funnel to indicate when someone has opted in. And so we're going to delete, we're going to delete ad recall, we're going to delete click through all, clicks all. We're going to leave click through rate link click, we're going to leave cost per click, we're going to leave this one. CPM is good impressions, relevant score, schedule we don't need. And reach results. Okay, great. So the other thing we want here is our landing page views because just because people linked clicked doesn't mean that they actually saw, they could have bounced. So we want to see that. And then if you have any other pages inside of your funnel, so for example maybe you're doing gamification and one of your landing pages is where they start playing the game, you'll want to add a standard event to that page too and bring it up inside of your reporting column and position it where it fits into the funnel. And that way you can see actually what's the flow through along the entire funnel. So let's go and save this as a preset and we'll go funnel leads. I don't know name this something that makes sense to you and then click apply. And every time you log into Facebook, you can even make this the set as default. I'm not going to do that right now because we're still just in the rapid fire testing round. But every time you come in here, you can just click on funnel leads and essentially it will pull up the information that you need to see. So right off the bat now I can see cost per clicks link click through rates. If I go down into the ad set again it shows me it's all saved. So I don't have to keep doing this every single time. It's easily accessible.
